title: Soothing Remedies for Itchy Eyes: 11 Top-Rated Herbal Creams

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for itchy eyes because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and ease redness in the eyes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis cream is a natural remedy that can help soothe itchy eyes. This cream is made from the flowers of the Calendula plant, which has been used for centuries to treat skin problems and irritations. When applied to itchy eyes, the cream helps to reduce inflammation and calm the skin around the eyes. The anti-inflammatory properties of Calendula officinalis can help to ease redness and swelling, making it easier to open and close the eyes without feeling like they're burning. The cream can also help to lock in moisture, which can help to soothe and calm dry, itchy eyes. Additionally, Calendula officinalis cream is free from harsh chemicals, making it a gentle and safe option for people with sensitive skin or eyes.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Hypericum perforatum herbal cream to help with itchy eyes, you will need dried St. John's Wort flowers, coconut oil, beeswax, and a small saucepan. First, infuse the dried flowers in the coconut oil by gently heating them together in a saucepan over low heat for about 30 minutes, or until the oil has turned a light golden color. Strain the oil through a cheesecloth into a clean container, and discard the solids. Next, melt the beeswax in a separate saucepan over low heat, then slowly add the infused coconut oil to the melted beeswax while constantly stirring. Continue to heat and stir until the mixture has thickened into a smooth, creamy consistency. Remove the saucepan from the heat and let the mixture cool for a few minutes before transferring it to a small jar or tin. The resulting cream can be applied directly to itchy eyes to help soothe and calm the irritation. Note: Before using the cream, perform a patch test on a small area of skin to check for any allergic reactions. Also, consult with a healthcare professional or a qualified herbalist before using Hypericum perforatum, especially if you are taking any medications or have any underlying health conditions.

remedies.remedy_desc: Symphytum officinale cream is used to help itchy eyes. This cream is made from the root of the comfrey plant. Comfrey has been used for a long time to treat skin problems, including itchy eyes. The cream works by reducing swelling and redness in the eyes. It also helps to calm the skin and stop the itching. When applied to the eyes, it can bring quick relief from itchy feelings. This is because it contains soothing compounds that help to quiet the skin. The cream is also gentle and won't irritate the eyes or skin, making it a good option for people with sensitive eyes. It's often used for minor eye irritations, such as dryness or irritation caused by allergies or dust.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Hypericum perforatum herbal cream that helps with itchy eyes, you will need some dried St John's Wort flowers, a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, beeswax, and a clean glass jar. First, steep a teaspoon of dried St John's Wort flowers in a tablespoon of carrier oil in a small saucepan over low heat for about 10 minutes. The mixture will start to release the herbal properties into the oil, making it infused. Next, melt a tablespoon of beeswax in the saucepan, then remove it from the heat and let it cool for a few minutes. Strain the infused oil mixture into the melted beeswax and stir well. Pour the mixture into a clean glass jar and let it cool completely before using it. Once cooled, you can apply a small amount to a cotton pad or a clean cloth and gently put it over your itchy eye to soothe the irritation. The Hypericum perforatum in the cream will help to reduce inflammation and itching due to its antihistamine and anti-inflammatory properties.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis cream can help with itchy eyes because it is soothing and calming. Aloe vera has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce redness and swelling in the eyes. When you put aloe vera cream on itchy eyes, it can cool and calm the skin, making the itching feel better. It also contains antioxidants that can help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals. This can help to reduce the itchiness and discomfort in the eyes. Additionally, aloe vera has natural moisturizing properties that can help to keep the eyes hydrated and healthy. This can be especially helpful if you have dry eyes or eye irritation.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Hypericum perforatum herbal creams that help with itchy eyes, start by gathering dried Hypericum perforatum flowers and leaves. You'll also need a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, beeswax, and a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water. Begin by mixing 1 part dried Hypericum perforatum flowers and leaves with 2 parts carrier oil in a clean glass jar. Let this mixture infuse in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily, until the oil has turned a light yellow color and has a herbal scent. After the infusion, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Next, melt 1-2 tablespoons of beeswax in the double boiler or heat-proof bowl, then slowly add 1-2 ounces of the infused oil, stirring constantly until the mixture has thickened into a smooth, creamy texture. Finally, pour the herbal cream into a clean glass jar and store it in the refrigerator to prevent spoilage. When you have itchy eyes, apply a small amount of this cream to the affected area to help soothe and calm the irritation.

remedies.remedy_desc: Euphorbia peplus cream is made from the sap of the Euphorbia peplus plant. This plant has been used for a long time to treat many skin problems. The sap in the cream has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. When you put the cream on itchy eyes, it helps to reduce the swelling and redness. This can make the itching feel better. The cream also has a soothing effect, which can calm the eyes and make them feel more comfortable. It's like a cool, calming balm for your eyes. remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Hypericum perforatum herbal cream that helps with itchy eyes, you will need a few simple ingredients. First, dry the Hypericum perforatum flowers and leaves by spreading them out in a single layer on a paper towel, then allowing them to air dry completely. Once dried, combine the dried plant material with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil in a clean glass jar. Let the mixture infuse in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every few days, until the oil has taken on the color and scent of the plant. After the infusion is complete, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean jar, discarding the solids. Next, mix the infused oil with a thickening agent like beeswax or shea butter to create a cream. Melt the beeswax or shea butter in a double boiler or in a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second increments, then slowly pour the infused oil into the melted wax or shea butter, stirring constantly until the mixture has thickened into a smooth, creamy consistency. Once the cream has cooled and set, transfer it to a clean jar or tin and store it in a cool, dark place. To use the cream, simply apply a small amount to the affected area with a gentle touch, ideally after washing your hands and face to minimize any potential irritation.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Hypericum perforatum herbal cream for itchy eyes, you will need dried Hypericum flowers, a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, beeswax, and a small saucepan. First, fill a clean glass jar with about 1/4 cup of dried Hypericum flowers, making sure they are fully covered with the carrier oil. Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking it every few days to help the oil extract the plant's medicinal properties. After this time, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container, discarding the solids. In a small saucepan, melt 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of the Hypericum oil over low heat, stirring until the wax is fully dissolved. Remove the saucepan from the heat and let the mixture cool and thicken into a creamy texture. Once cooled, transfer the cream to a clean container and store it in the refrigerator to help keep it fresh. Apply a small amount of this Hypericum cream to your itchy eyes with a clean cotton swab to soothe and calm the irritation.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams can help with itchy eyes because it has anti-inflammatory properties. When your eyes get itchy, it's usually because of an allergic reaction or irritation. This cream can calm down the skin around your eyes and reduce swelling. The anti-inflammatory properties in Echinacea purpurea can also help to reduce the redness and itching caused by an allergic reaction. The cream is often made from the plant's roots and flowers, which have been used for centuries to help soothe skin irritations. remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Hypericum perforatum herbal creams that help with itchy eyes, first you need to prepare the herb. Dry the St John's Wort (Hypericum perforatum) flowers and leaves by spreading them on a paper bag or a clean cloth in a dry, dark place. Once dry, grind the flowers and leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or a mortar and pestle. Next, mix 2-3 tablespoons of the powder with 2-3 tablespoons of a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil in a small bowl. Heat the mixture in a double boiler or a saucepan with a bowl on top, gently warming it for 10-15 minutes, or until the oil becomes warm and the powder dissolves. Remove the bowl from the heat and let the mixture cool down. Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean bowl, pressing on the solids to get all the liquid out. Transfer the liquid into a glass jar and add a few drops of a preservative like vitamin E oil to prevent it from going bad. Finally, mix 1-2 tablespoons of the herbal oil with 1-2 tablespoons of a thickening agent like beeswax and shea butter, and heat the mixture again in a double boiler until the beeswax melts. Pour the mixture into a clean glass jar and let it cool down to form a thick and creamy cream that can be applied to itchy eyes to soothe and calm them.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Hypericum perforatum herbal cream to soothe itchy eyes, first you need to dry the Hypericum flowers. Gently spread the flowers on a paper towel, allowing air to dry them completely. Once dry, grind the flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle. remedies.remedy_desc: Plantago lanceolata cream is a natural remedy for itchy eyes. The cream is made from the leaf of the Plantago lanceolata plant, also known as ribwort or buckthorn. remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Hypericum perforatum herbal cream that helps with itchy eyes, start by drying and preparing the St. John's Wort flowers, which contain hypericin and hyperforin, the active ingredients that soothe and calm irritated eyes. First, harvest a bunch of fresh St. John's Wort flowers on a sunny day and dry them thoroughly in a warm, dark place, such as a cupboard or a paper bag. Once the flowers are completely dry, you can use a coffee grinder or a mortar and pestle to finely chop them into a powder. Next, mix the St. John's Wort powder with a carrier oil like olive or coconut oil in a ratio of about 1:5, then heat the mixture in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in short bursts until it reaches a temperature of around 150-180 degrees Fahrenheit. Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool, then add a thickening agent like beeswax or candelilla wax to help it thicken into a creamy texture. Finally, pour the mixture into small jars and let it cool completely before using the Hypericum perforatum herbal cream to soothe and calm itchy eyes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum creams, also known as St. John's Wort, contain a chemical called hyperforin. This chemical has anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties, which can help to reduce swelling and itching in itchy eyes. The hyperforin in the cream can block the release of histamine, a substance in the body that causes itching and swelling. As a result, the itching and redness in itchy eyes can be reduced, allowing the eyes to feel more comfortable. The cream can also help to soothe and calm the skin around the eyes, reducing the risk of infection and promoting healing.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Hypericum perforatum herbal cream to help with itchy eyes, you will need dried Hypericum perforatum flowers, a carrier oil such as olive or coconut oil, beeswax, and a double boiler. First, mix 2-3 tablespoons of dried Hypericum perforatum flowers with 1/4 cup of carrier oil in a clean glass jar. Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day, to allow the flowers to infuse into the oil. After the infusion process, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Next, melt 1-2 tablespoons of beeswax in the double boiler. Add the infused oil to the melted beeswax and mix well. Continue to heat the mixture until it reaches a smooth, creamy consistency. Remove the double boiler from the heat and let the mixture cool until it reaches room temperature. intro_desc: If you have itchy eyes, you know how annoying it can be. Itchy eyes can make you rub them, which can make the problem worse and even lead to infections. Using herbal creams is a good way to soothe itchy eyes. Two herbs that are often used in these creams are Calendula officinalis and Symphytum officinale. Calendula is good for itchy eyes because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help calm the skin and reduce redness. It also has antiseptic properties, which help prevent infections. Symphytum, on the other hand, is a natural moisturizer that helps keep the skin hydrated and healthy. This can help reduce itchiness and make your eyes feel more comfortable.  Fixing itchy eyes is important because it can help you feel more relaxed and focused. When your eyes are itchy, you may be tempted to rub them, which can make the problem worse. This can lead to a cycle of itchiness and rubbing, which can be hard to break. By using herbal creams with Calendula and Symphytum, you can help soothe your itchy eyes and break this cycle. This can improve your life in many ways. You'll be able to focus better at work or school, and you'll be able to enjoy your favorite activities without feeling uncomfortable. You'll also be able to get a good night's sleep without waking up with itchy eyes. By taking care of your itchy eyes, you can take care of yourself and feel more confident and relaxed.  Herbal creams with Calendula and Symphytum are a good option for itchy eyes because they are natural and gentle. They don't contain harsh chemicals or artificial ingredients that can irritate the skin. Instead, they use natural ingredients that are gentle and effective. This makes them a good choice for people who want to soothe itchy eyes without using harsh chemicals. By using herbal creams with Calendula and Symphytum, you can help soothe your itchy eyes and improve your life.
supplementary_best: To remedy itchy eyes, a combination of herbal creams is more effective than using a single herb. A blend of  Aloe barbadensis and Echinacea purpurea provides relief from itchy eyes. Aloe barbadensis soothes and calms the skin around the eyes, reducing inflammation and cooling the area. Echinacea purpurea has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce redness and swelling associated with itchy eyes. The combination of these two herbs also enhances the skin's natural barrier function, providing long-lasting relief from itchy eyes.  Another herb, Euphrasia officinalis, also known as eyebright, is added to this combination to target the root cause of itchy eyes, which is often due to allergies or irritants. Euphrasia officinalis has anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties that help to reduce itching and redness caused by allergic reactions. This herb also has a natural decongestant effect that helps to clear out any irritants or allergens from the eyes.  A small amount of Hypericum perforatum, also known as St. John's Wort, is added to this combination to enhance its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Hypericum perforatum helps to reduce inflammation and promote the healing of the skin around the eyes, further reducing the itching and redness.  This combination of Aloe barbadensis, Echinacea purpurea, Euphrasia officinalis, and Hypericum perforatum provides a comprehensive remedy for itchy eyes, addressing both the symptoms and the root cause of the issue. The soothing and calming properties of Aloe barbadensis, combined with the anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties of Echinacea purpurea and Euphrasia officinalis, provide long-lasting relief from itchy eyes. The addition of Hypericum perforatum enhances the anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of the combination, promoting the healing and soothing of the skin around the eyes.
supplementary_avoid: If you have itchy eyes, there are some herbal creams you should avoid. One of these is cream containing Eyebright's relative, 'Verbascum thapsus', also known as Mullein. Mullein can be too drying and irritating for the skin around your eyes, making the itchiness worse. Another one to avoid is cream with 'Arctium lappa' or Burdock root. Burdock can cause skin irritation and allergic reactions, which can lead to more itchiness and discomfort. 'Taraxacum officinale' or Dandelion root cream is also not recommended, as it can cause skin problems and make itchiness worse. These creams may seem helpful for soothing itchy eyes, but they can actually make the problem worse.
supplementary_other_remedies: When itchy eyes are a problem, there are several natural remedies that can help. Warm compresses are a simple and effective solution. Applying a warm washcloth to the eyes for a few minutes can help loosen and remove any dirt or debris that may be causing the itchiness. Cold compresses, on the other hand, can help reduce swelling and ease the itching. A cold, damp washcloth or an ice pack wrapped in a cloth can be applied to the eyes for a few minutes at a time. Some people also find relief from itchy eyes by splashing their eyes with water or using a neti pot to rinse out any irritants. This can be especially helpful when allergies are the cause of the itchiness. Another remedy is to try to reduce the amount of allergens or irritants that are getting into the eyes. This can be done by using a mask when outdoors, wearing sunglasses to protect the eyes from dust and pollen, and avoiding rubbing or touching the eyes, which can spread allergens and irritants around.
supplementary_related_ailments: People who experience itchy eyes often have other health issues related to allergies or irritants in their environment. They may be more prone to having sinus pressure and congestion due to inflammation caused by allergens like pollen or dust. The medicinal herb quercetin, found in plants like oak and maple trees, can help reduce inflammation and relieve sinus pressure. Quercetin is a natural antihistamine that blocks the release of chemical histamine, which causes allergic reactions.  Itchy eyes can also be a symptom of conjunctivitis, an eye infection caused by bacteria or viruses. People with itchy eyes may also experience dry eyes, which can be treated with herbs like eyebright and goldenseal. Eyebright has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and redness in the eyes, while goldenseal has antimicrobial properties that can help fight off bacterial infections.  People with itchy eyes may also experience skin issues like eczema or dermatitis, which can be treated with herbs like aloe vera and chamomile. Aloe vera has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm irritated skin, while chamomile has antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness and itching.  In addition, people with itchy eyes may also experience respiratory issues like asthma or bronchitis, which can be treated with herbs like ephedra and ginkgo biloba. Ephedra has decongestant properties that can help relieve nasal congestion, while ginkgo biloba has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the lungs.  Herbal remedies like these can provide relief from itchy eyes and other associated health issues. They work by reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and soothing irritated skin and eyes.
